case_studies.title: LTCM Collapse (1998)

thesis.fragility: 25:1 leverage, convergence trades assumed normal distributions
thesis.hubris: Nobel laureates believed they had eliminated risk
thesis.outcome: Lost $4.6B in weeks, required Fed-orchestrated bailout

taleb_analysis.correlation: Assets became perfectly correlated in crisis

lessons: Models that ignore fat tails will eventually fail catastrophically
Leverage transforms temporary losses into permanent destruction
Correlations break down exactly when you need diversification most

case_studies.title: Universa's COVID Crash Return (2020)

thesis.strategy: Persistent tail hedging with deep OTM puts
thesis.cost: Accepts 'bleed' of ~2-3% annually in normal times

lessons: Tail hedges can transform portfolio outcomes
Accept small consistent costs for asymmetric protection
Don't try to time when to hedge - always be hedged
Convex payoffs are worth paying for

case_studies.title: Taleb's 2008 Portfolio Protection

thesis.philosophy: Long-term out-of-the-money options for tail protection
thesis.approach: Barbell of treasury bonds + tail hedges

antifragile_principles.survival_first: Never risk ruin for incremental gains
antifragile_principles.convexity: Structure positions with limited downside, unlimited upside
antifragile_principles.optionality: Pay for options, don't sell them

lessons: Fragile systems appear stable until they suddenly collapse
Protection is cheapest when no one wants it
Antifragile portfolios get stronger from volatility
Via negativa: What you avoid matters more than what you do
